item.title: Cron expressions not validated — invalid expressions like 99 99 99 99 99 accepted silently
item.intent: All cron expressions must be validated before use. Invalid cron schedules must be caught at config generation time, not at runtime when jobs silently fail to run.
item.description: Code review found that src/design/configure/generate.ts normalizeCronExpr() tries to fix step syntax but never validates the expression. Invalid fields (minute > 59, hour > 23, etc.) pass through. Additionally, src/operate/doctor/checks.ts cron-syntax check only looks for step patterns, not field count or value ranges.
steps: Add a cron validation function (use cron-parser library or manual range checks)
Integrate validation into normalizeCronExpr() in generate.ts
Update doctor cron-syntax check to validate field count and ranges
Add tests for boundary values and invalid expressions
acceptanceCriteria: Cron expressions are validated for correct field count (5 fields)
Field value ranges are validated (minute 0-59, hour 0-23, etc.)
Invalid cron expressions produce clear error messages at config generation time
Doctor cron-syntax check validates field ranges, not just step patterns
